etymology.description: 廣東(廣東) (Guǎngdōng, 'Guangdong') refers to the Guangdong province in southern China, which is historically the region where Cantonese is spoken. 話(話) (huà, 'speech; language; dialect') indicates a form of spoken language. Thus, 廣東話(廣東話) refers to the language or dialect spoken in the Guangdong region, known as Cantonese.
etymology.interpretation: 廣東話(廣東話) (Guǎngdōnghuà, 'Cantonese') is the dialect of Chinese that originated in the Guangdong region. The term specifies the local language as opposed to other dialects of Chinese.

parts.english: Guangdong; a province in southern China

examples.chinese: 我正在学习广东话(我正在學習廣東話)。
在香港(在香港)，很多人说广东话(很多人說廣東話)。
examples.pinyin: Zài Xiānggǎng, hěn duō rén shuō Guǎngdōnghuà.
examples.english: In Hong Kong, many people speak Cantonese.

examples.chinese: 她能流畅地讲广东话(她能流暢地講廣東話)。
examples.pinyin: Tā néng liúchàng de jiǎng Guǎngdōnghuà.
